title: First 20 Linux Commands
tools_required: Linux terminal (Kali, Parrot, or any distro)
when_to_use: Foundational skill for all terminal-based offensive work

steps.title: pwd
steps.tip: Always know where you are. Run pwd whenever you're unsure

steps.title: ls -la
steps.instruction: ls -la — list all files with details (including hidden)
steps.tip: The -a flag shows hidden files (dotfiles). The -l flag shows permissions, ownership, and size

steps.title: cd
steps.tip: cd ~ goes home. cd - goes to previous directory. cd .. goes up one level

steps.title: cat
steps.tip: For large files, use less or head/tail instead. cat is best for small files

steps.title: grep
steps.tip: Add -i for case-insensitive, -r for recursive directory search, -n for line numbers

steps.title: find
steps.tip: Use 2>/dev/null to suppress permission errors. -type f for files only, -type d for directories

steps.title: chmod
steps.tip: 755 = owner rwx, group rx, others rx. 600 = owner rw only. Know the common patterns

steps.title: File Operations
steps.instruction: cp, mv, rm — copy, move, delete files
steps.tip: rm -rf is irreversible. Always double-check paths before deletion. Use -i for confirmation prompts

steps.title: man
steps.instruction: man command — read manual page for any command
steps.tip: Press / to search within man pages. Press q to quit. man is your offline documentation

steps.title: history
steps.tip: Use !number to re-run a command. Ctrl+R for reverse search. Clear with history -c on ops
